title: NBA Daily Fastbreak: May 28, 2026
subtitle: Your daily dose of hoops action and headlines!

items.headline: Knicks Sweep Cavs, Punch Ticket to NBA Finals!
items.summary: The New York Knicks are officially headed to the NBA Finals after a dominant 4-0 sweep of the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference Finals. Jalen Brunson, named Conference Finals MVP, led the charge for the red-hot Knicks.

items.headline: Thunder Take 3-2 Lead in West Finals After Game 5 Thriller!
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder are just one win away from reaching the NBA Finals, taking a commanding 3-2 series lead over the San Antonio Spurs with a 127-114 victory in Game 5 of the Western Conference Finals. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander once again led the Thunder offense.

items.headline: Lakers Eye Malik Monk Reunion to Form 'Dangerous Luka Doncic Duo'
items.summary: Rumors are swirling that the Los Angeles Lakers are targeting a reunion with Sacramento Kings guard Malik Monk in a potential trade involving Rui Hachimura and Dalton Knecht, aiming to build a formidable backcourt alongside Luka Doncic.

items.headline: OKC Dominates Game 5 to Take Series Lead!
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder put on an offensive clinic, scoring 82 points by early in the third quarter, en route to a crucial Game 5 victory over the San Antonio Spurs.

items.headline: Rookie McCain Scores 20 in Playoff Start!
Wemby's 20 Points Not Enough in Game 5
Deni Avdija Eyeing Blazers Extension
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers' rising star Deni Avdija (ISR) is reportedly in discussions for a contract extension as the team looks to secure his future after a season where he averaged 13.3 points, 6.3 rebounds, and 3.4 assists.

items.headline: Joe Mazzulla Named NBA Coach of the Year!
items.summary: Boston Celtics Head Coach Joe Mazzulla earned the NBA Coach of the Year award for the 2025-26 season, leading his team to the No. 2 seed in the East despite key injuries, including Jayson Tatum's Achilles surgery.

items.headline: League Awards Roundup: SGA MVP, Wemby DPOY, Flagg ROY!
items.summary: The NBA has announced its major awards for the 2025-26 season: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (OKC) won MVP and Clutch Player of the Year, Victor Wembanyama (SAS) took home Defensive Player of the Year, and Cooper Flagg (DAL) was named Rookie of the Year.

items.headline: LeBron James Reportedly Awaiting Lakers' Future Plan for Extension Talks
items.summary: LeBron James and his agent Rich Paul are reportedly waiting for the Los Angeles Lakers to present a clear plan for the team's future and a max contract offer before engaging in extension discussions.

items.headline: OKC Exploring Trade Options for No. 17 Draft Pick
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder are reportedly open to trading their No. 17 pick in the upcoming NBA Draft, with all 15 of their current players under contract for next season, indicating potential roster adjustments.

items.headline: Jalen Williams Questionable for WCF Game 6 with Hamstring Injury
items.summary: Oklahoma City Thunder's Jalen Williams is listed as questionable for tonight's crucial Western Conference Finals Game 6 against the Spurs due to a left hamstring injury.

items.headline: WCF Game 6: Thunder Look to Close Out Spurs!
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs host the Oklahoma City Thunder in a pivotal Game 6 of the Western Conference Finals. The Thunder lead the series 3-2 and will look to clinch their spot in the NBA Finals.
